skill.name: frontend-design
skill.description: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with strong visual direction, responsive behavior, accessibility details, and polished interaction design.
skill.summary: Guide high-quality frontend interface design across typography, color, layout, motion, interaction, and UX writing.
skill.author: pbakaus
skill.license: Apache 2.0. Based on Anthropic's frontend-design skill. See NOTICE.md for attribution.

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, weak-cryptography, keylogger, and reconnaissance patterns, but manual review found these are Markdown syntax, CSS examples, design terminology, and file-extension false positives. The only retained risk evidence is four external documentation links in reference files; no evidence found of prompt injection, credential access, executable code, or data exfiltration.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Examples Flagged as Commands
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Static command-execution alerts come from Markdown code fences and inline CSS or HTML examples, not executable scripts. Confidence: 0.96. Confidence reasoning: The reviewed files are Markdown references with no shell script, Ruby code, process spawning, or user-input command path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is documentation text and fenced examples only. No executable command handler or runtime file was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Security Keywords Used as Design Guidance
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Weak-cryptography, keylogger, and reconnaissance alerts are triggered by terms such as Markdown file names, CSS properties, keystroke guidance, and accessibility wording. Confidence: 0.95. Confidence reasoning: Manual review found design advice, not cryptographic code, key capture, or system inspection behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious words appear inside prose about forms, typography, responsive testing, and Markdown content. No evidence found of cryptography, keylogging, or reconnaissance implementation.

low_findings.title: External Documentation Links Present
low_findings.description: Verdict: LOW_RISK_TRUE_POSITIVE for hardcoded URLs, but FALSE_POSITIVE for active network behavior. The links point to public design and accessibility resources and are not fetched by code. Confidence: 0.9. Confidence reasoning: The URLs are visible Markdown links; no executable network request logic was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network indicator is real because URLs are present, but they are documentation references only. There is no fetch, curl, dependency installer, or exfiltration path.

content.value_statement: Generic AI-generated interfaces often look repetitive and miss accessibility details. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward distinctive, responsive, production-ready frontend work.
actual_capabilities: Guides selection of a clear visual direction before implementation begins.
Provides typography, spacing, color, contrast, and theme guidance for web interfaces.
Recommends accessible interaction patterns for focus states, forms, modals, and keyboard navigation.
Covers responsive layout techniques, container queries, safe areas, and responsive images.
Advises motion timing, easing, reduced-motion support, and perceived performance details.
Improves UX writing for labels, errors, empty states, loading states, and confirmations.
limitations: Does not run automated visual tests, browser checks, or accessibility audits by itself.
Does not provide a reusable component library or framework-specific implementation package.
External reference links depend on third-party sites remaining available.
Needs product context, brand constraints, and target users for best results.

use_cases.title: Design a Product Interface
use_cases.description: Create a polished application screen with clear hierarchy, responsive behavior, and production-ready design details.

use_cases.title: Improve Frontend Implementation Quality
use_cases.description: Turn a basic component or page into a refined interface with better spacing, states, motion, and accessibility.

use_cases.title: Create Better AI Design Prompts
use_cases.description: Guide Claude, Codex, or Claude Code with stronger design constraints before generating frontend work.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Polished Page
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a single page or simple component.
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a production-ready frontend page for [product or use case]. Choose a clear visual direction, define the audience, and include responsive layout, accessible interactions, and refined typography.

prompt_templates.title: Refine an Existing Component
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for improving a current interface.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this frontend component and improve its design quality. Focus on visual hierarchy, spacing rhythm, color contrast, focus states, empty states, and UX writing while preserving the intended behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Responsive App Flow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for multiple connected states.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a responsive app flow for [workflow]. Include desktop and mobile adaptations, loading and error states, keyboard navigation, reduced-motion behavior, and concise UI copy for every important state.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Full Interface Direction
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for design critique and redesign planning.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this interface for generic AI design patterns, accessibility gaps, weak hierarchy, inconsistent motion, and unclear UX writing. Propose a stronger visual direction and then implement the highest-impact improvements.

output_examples.input: A basic SaaS dashboard needs a more memorable first screen.
output: A clear aesthetic direction for the dashboard audience.
A refined layout plan with hierarchy, spacing, and responsive behavior.
Improved states for loading, empty data, errors, and primary actions.

output_examples.input: A mobile checkout form feels generic and hard to use.
output: Field labels, validation timing, and error messages rewritten for clarity.
Touch-friendly spacing and visible focus behavior across inputs and buttons.
Recommendations for safer confirmation, undo, and loading patterns.

output_examples.input: A landing page looks like a common AI-generated template.
output: A stronger visual concept tied to the product and audience.
More distinctive typography, color, imagery, and layout choices.
A practical implementation checklist for design polish.

best_practices: Start with audience, purpose, tone, constraints, and one memorable design idea.
Ask for responsive behavior, accessibility states, and UX copy as part of the same task.
Provide brand, content, and framework constraints before requesting implementation.
anti_patterns: Requesting a generic modern interface without product context or target users.
Using cards, gradients, glowing dark themes, or repeated icon blocks as default design choices.
Treating mobile as a smaller desktop layout instead of adapting the workflow.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create polished frontend interfaces, pages, components, and app screens with stronger design direction.

faq.question: Does it generate working frontend code?
faq.answer: It guides the AI to generate working frontend code when the user asks for implementation.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it replace accessibility testing?
faq.answer: No. It includes accessibility guidance, but projects still need real testing with browsers and assistive technology.

faq.question: Can it improve an existing design?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide critique and refinement of typography, layout, color, motion, interaction, and writing.

faq.question: Are the external links required for the skill to work?
faq.answer: No. They are reference links in documentation, not runtime dependencies.
